name: Cresta del Gallo and Murcia Loop
name_original: Santuario de Fuensanta y subida al Mirador Cresta del Gallo desde Algezares — circular desde Los Jerónimos
description: Cycle the moderate 23.2-mile Cresta del Gallo and Murcia Loop, featuring a challenging climb and scenic views in the Murcia region.
region_intro: Road cycling around Rio Segura offers diverse terrain in southeastern Spain, centered on the Segura River. The region features flat greenways along the river, passing through fertile agricultural lands with citrus groves and historical water wheels. More challenging routes extend into areas like the Valle de Ricote and the Sierra Espuña, providing significant elevation gains and varied landscapes. The area is characterized by river valleys, orchards, and hills, catering to different cycling preferences. Best road cycling routes around Rio Segura The most popular road cycling route is Puerto Cabezo de la Plata and Puerto del Garruchal — return from Murcia, a 27.4 miles (44.0 km) trail that takes 2 hours 9 minutes to complete. This moderate route includes climbs over hills and offers views of the surrounding landscape. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Ricote Valley and Quípar Reservoir Loop, a difficult 69.6 miles (112.1 km) path. This route traverses the diverse terrain of the Ricote Valley and passes by the Quípar Reservoir. Local road cyclists also love the Tour of Barrancos de Gebas from Alcantarilla, a 31.5 miles (50.7 km) trail leading through the unique badlands of Barrancos de Gebas, often completed in about 2 hours 29 minutes. Road cycling around Rio Segura is defined by river valleys, agricultural lands, and hills, with routes ranging from flat riverside paths to challenging climbs. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy greenways to difficult mountain ascents. The routes in Rio Segura are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 150 reviews. More than 13,000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Rio Segura's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Rio Segura?
A: There are over 500 road cycling routes in the Rio Segura region, offering a wide variety of options for different skill levels and preferences. The komoot community has rated these routes highly, with an average score of 4.2 stars.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Rio Segura?
A: The Rio Segura region enjoys a Mediterranean climate, making it ideal for cycling for much of the year. The best times are typically from &lt;strong&gt;February to June&lt;/strong&gt; and &lt;strong&gt;September to November&lt;/strong&gt;, when the weather is mild and sunny. While summers can be very hot, the cooler months often allow for comfortable rides.

Q: Are there easy road cycling routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, the Rio Segura region offers several easy, flat routes, particularly along the Segura River. These greenways are perfect for beginners and families. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1008712349/murcia-and-alcantarilla-via-the-northwest-greenway-and-the-segura-river-circular-from-molina-de-segura" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Murcia and Alcantarilla via the Northwest Greenway and the Segura River — circular from Molina de Segura&lt;/a&gt;, which is an easy, mostly flat option.

Q: Where can I find challenging road cycling routes with significant climbs?
A: For more challenging rides, head towards areas like the Sierra Espuña Regional Park or the Valle de Ricote. These regions feature significant elevation gains and diverse terrains. A difficult route traversing the Ricote Valley is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1008393804/ricote-valley-and-quipar-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ricote Valley and Quípar Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of landscapes and natural features will I encounter while cycling in Rio Segura?
A: You'll experience a diverse range of landscapes, from lush orchards and fertile agricultural lands along the Segura River to the more arid, dramatic badlands of Barrancos de Gebas and the forested mountains of Sierra Espuña. Many routes offer views of historical elements like ancient water wheels and dams.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in the region?
A: Yes, many routes in Rio Segura are designed as circular loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. An example of a moderate circular route is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1008702715/embalse-de-la-pradera-y-orihuela-circular-from-murcia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de la Pradera y Orihuela — circular from Murcia&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or attractions along the cycling routes?
A: Along the routes, you can discover charming villages and historical sites. In the broader region, you might find attractions like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2925673" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Murcia Cathedral&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3833926" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Real Casino of Murcia&lt;/a&gt;. For mountain passes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2489791" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Garruchal Pass&lt;/a&gt; is a notable feature.

Q: Can I find routes that pass through unique geological formations?
A: Absolutely. The region features unique geological formations, such as the badlands of Barrancos de Gebas. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1009652950/tour-of-barrancos-de-gebas-from-alcantarilla" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tour of Barrancos de Gebas from Alcantarilla&lt;/a&gt; offers a moderate ride through this distinctive landscape.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Rio Segura?
A: The komoot community highly rates road cycling in Rio Segura, with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 150 reviews. Cyclists often praise the region's diverse terrain, ranging from flat riverside paths to challenging mountain ascents, and the opportunity to explore charming villages and historical sites.

Q: Are there routes that explore the agricultural heritage of the region?
A: Many routes will take you through the fertile agricultural lands, particularly citrus groves, which are irrigated by the Segura River using ancient Moorish canals and water wheels. The lush vegetation along the riverbanks provides a refreshing backdrop to these rides.

Q: Are there any routes that include reservoir views?
A: Yes, some routes incorporate views of local reservoirs. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1008393804/ricote-valley-and-quipar-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ricote Valley and Quípar Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, passes by the Quípar Reservoir, offering scenic water views amidst diverse terrain.

Q: Can I find routes that connect different towns or cities?
A: Yes, several routes connect various towns and cities, allowing for longer journeys or rides between urban centers. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1008668306/beniel-along-the-segura-river-circular-from-murcia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Beniel along the Segura River — circular from Murcia&lt;/a&gt; connects Murcia with Beniel, following the river.
tips: Sanctuary of the Virgen de la Fuensanta, patron saint of Murcia. Popular area of passage for bicycle lovers. The Sanctuary is located 5.5 km from the city of Murcia and surrounded by nature with magnificent views of the Huerta del Segura.

It can be climbed by two variants, from Algezares (5.5 km and 369 of unevenness) or from San José (6.20 km and 382 of unevenness). Both will travel through a unique place with beautiful views of Murcia. The final part from San José, before, was broken but now offers asphalt in perfect condition. The name of the Cresta del Gallo comes from the peculiar shape of the mountain to which we are heading. Going up from San José we will go down to the Virgen de la Fuensanta Sanctuary, patron saint of Murcia.
One of the bridges that connects the two banks of the Segura River as it passes through the Murcian capital. An ideal place to ride a bike and/or be in contact with nature.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle the Cresta del Gallo and Murcia Loop?
faqs.answer: The Mediterranean climate in the Murcia region makes spring and autumn ideal for cycling this route, offering pleasant temperatures and abundant sunshine. Winters are generally mild, but summers can be quite hot, so if you plan to ride in summer, it's best to start early in the morning or late in the afternoon to avoid the peak heat.

faqs.question: Is the Cresta del Gallo climb challenging for road cyclists?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Cresta del Gallo is known as a demanding climb. From Los Garres, it's approximately 5.5 km long with an average gradient of 6.8% and a 379-meter elevation gain. You'll encounter sections with double-digit gradients, making it a punchy and rewarding ascent. The overall route difficulty can range from moderate to hard, depending on your fitness level.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this route?
faqs.answer: The route features varied terrain, including quiet roads, some main roads, and dedicated cycleways. While the Cresta del Gallo climb itself is paved, some sections, especially on descents, might have less maintained surfaces or potholes. You'll cycle through diverse landscapes, from the winding roads into the mountains to more arid, brown, and yellow scenery around areas like Cabezo de la Plata.

faqs.question: What are the main highlights and viewpoints along the loop?
faqs.answer: This loop offers several notable sights. You'll pass the beautiful Sanctuary of the Virgin of La Fuensanta. From the top of Cresta del Gallo, you'll be treated to sensational views across the plains, and on clear days, you might even spot the Mar Menor from the largest monolith, "La Panocha." The route also includes the La Fica Bridge.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Cresta del Gallo and Murcia Loop?
faqs.answer: The tour description indicates a circular route starting from Los Jerónimos. For parking, you'll typically find options in the surrounding towns or near the start point in Los Jerónimos. It's advisable to look for public parking areas or designated spots in these urban centers before heading out on your ride.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required to cycle in the Parque Regional de Carrascoy y El Valle?
faqs.answer: No, there are generally no specific permits or entrance fees required for cycling on public roads and trails within the Parque Regional de Carrascoy y El Valle, where the Cresta del Gallo is located. However, always respect local regulations, stay on marked paths, and be mindful of the natural environment.

faqs.question: Is the Cresta del Gallo and Murcia Loop suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Given the demanding nature of the Cresta del Gallo climb, with its significant gradients, this route is generally considered to require good to very good fitness and advanced riding skills. While parts of the loop might be more manageable, the main climb makes it less suitable for absolute beginners. There are other, flatter routes in the Murcia region that might be a better starting point for novice cyclists.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other cycling or hiking trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route shares sections with several other official trails. You'll find yourself on parts of the Camino de Santiago del Azahar, Camino del Apóstol, and the Río Segura trail. It also intersects with the Transmurciana 01 and the specific cycling climb route, [CIMA MU05] Cresta del Gallo * Algezares.

faqs.question: Is the route dog-friendly or suitable for families with children?
faqs.answer: This route is primarily designed for road cycling and includes challenging climbs and sections on public roads. While dogs are generally allowed in the regional park, this specific cycling loop is not typically recommended as dog-friendly for accompanying pets due to the nature of the activity and terrain. Similarly, its difficulty makes it less suitable for families with young children unless they are experienced junior cyclists.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a ride on this loop?
faqs.answer: Given the challenging nature and potential for varied weather, it's essential to bring plenty of water, especially during warmer months. Sun protection (sunscreen, sunglasses, cap), appropriate cycling gear, a repair kit, and snacks are highly recommended. A fully charged phone and a GPS device with the route loaded are also crucial for navigation and safety.

faqs.question: Are there places to stop for food or drinks along the way?
faqs.answer: While the route takes you through some natural and quieter areas, you'll pass through or near towns like Algezares and Los Jerónimos, especially at the start and end of the loop. These areas typically offer cafes, restaurants, and shops where you can refuel and grab refreshments. It's always a good idea to carry some snacks and water, especially for the more remote sections of the climb.

View of Murcia from Cresta del Gallo
